During the Cold War, several leaders held power in different countries. In the United States, Presidents Truman, Eisenhower, Kennedy, Johnson, Nixon, Ford, Carter, Reagan, and Bush Sr. were in charge at various times. In the Soviet Union, leaders like Stalin, Khrushchev, Brezhnev, Andropov, Chernenko, and Gorbachev held power. These individuals shaped global politics through tensions and strategies. The Cold War involved a tense standoff between two superpowers and their allies. This period was marked by proxy wars, arms races, and ideological battles. The leadership in both nations played a crucial role in maintaining this balance of power. The Cold War ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in the early 1990s.
